title: Sick-Day Smoky Yeast Stir-fry
description: A fast action yeast-based dish with a smoky and caramelized flavor profile, perfect for sick days due to its low-fat-friendly characteristics.

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons low-sodium soy sauce
ingredients.notes: Replace with coconut aminos for a vegan option.

ingredients.name: 1 cup quick-rise yeast blend (active dry)
ingredients.notes: Use 1/2 teaspoon active dry yeast instead for a quicker start.

ingredients.name: 2 cups mixed vegetables (e.g., bell peppers, onions, carrots)
Juice of 1 lemon (optional for a tangy twist)

instructions: In a small saucepan, combine soy sauce and olive oil. Warm over medium heat until just simmering.
Add minced garlic to the warm liquid mixture and cook for about 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Gradually add yeast blend into the garlic-sauce mixture, whisking well to incorporate evenly.
Slowly stir in mixed vegetables and chopped cashews. Continue cooking over low heat until all ingredients are heated through and flavors meld together.
Season with lemon juice or a pinch of salt if needed. Garnish with fresh parsley before serving.
Serve hot as a light snack or side dish.
